Ukraine Conflict News





06 February 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • National Defense Areas expanded, established along Texas border US Air Force 06 Feb 2026 -- The Department of the Air Force is expanding existing and establishing new National Defense Areas (NDAs) along the Texas border in support of the Department of War's ongoing mission to secure the southern border.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Statement to the Conference on Disarmament US Dept. of State 06 Feb 2026 -- Remarks ny Thomas G. DiNanno, Under Secretary (T), Under Secretary for Arms Control and International Security
  • Washington Seeks New Path On Nuclear Arms As Treaty With Russia Expires RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- The New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START), the final remaining bilateral agreement limiting US and Russian nuclear arsenals, expired on February 5, closing a chapter that began in the Cold War and survived its end.
  • US to Maintain Robust Nuclear Arsenal for Peace and Strength - US Under Secretary of State Sputnik 06 Feb 2026 -- The United States will maintain its nuclear deterrent arsenal in order to be able to negotiate from the position of force, US Under Secretary of State for Arms Control and International Security Thomas DiNanno said on Friday.
  • China calls on US to resume strategic stability dialogue with Russia on arms reduction treaty: FM responding to Trump's proposal of new nuclear arms deal Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- In response to a question regarding US President Donald Trump's proposal of a "new and more comprehensive" strategic arms agreement with Russia instead of renewing the New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START), Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Friday that China is not aware of the specific details, and that with a view of safeguarding global strategic stability in the long run, China calls on the US to resume strategic stability dialogue with Russia and discuss follow‑up arrangements for the New START upon its expiration. This is also the shared expectation of the international community, Lin said.
  • Lapse of U.S.-Russia treaty leaves nuclear arms control in dangerous vacuum People's Daily 06 Feb 2026 -- The White House has allowed the U.S.-Russia New Strategic Arms Reduction Treaty (New START) to formally expire on Thursday, ushering in a new era of nuclear uncertainty amid growing fears of a renewed arms race.

  • ESTABLISHING AN AMERICA FIRST ARMS TRANSFER STRATEGY The White House 06 Feb 2026
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Establishes the America First Arms Transfer Strategy The White House 06 Feb 2026
  • War Department Cuts Ties With Harvard University DoD News 06 Feb 2026 -- Secretary of War Pete Hegseth today announced the War Department would sever its academic ties with Harvard University, because attendance at the school no longer meets the needs of the War Department or the military services.
  • Marines Establish Refueling Point in Indo-Pacific DoD News 06 Feb 2026 -- Marines assigned to Marine Wing Support Squadron 171 and Marine Fighter Attack Squadron 232 traveled to Tinian, one of the Northern Mariana Islands, to establish and operate a forward arming and refueling point during an aviation training relocation program aimed at developing expeditionary aviation capabilities and ensuring security throughout the Indo-Pacific region.
  • As Promised, War Department Moving Out Fast on Drone Dominance DoD News 06 Feb 2026 -- The War Department announced Feb. 3 the selection of 25 vendors who will help the department achieve its goal of getting some 300,000 drones into the force, both quickly and inexpensively, by 2027.
  • US civil affairs Soldiers, Kenyan partners strengthen relationships with a medical camp Army News 06 Feb 2026 -- Soldiers assigned to Civil Affairs Team Kenya, Alpha Company, Civil Affairs Battalion, U.S. Army Southern European Task Force, Africa (SETAF-AF) engaged with multiple Kenyan civilian and military organizations to increase interoperability by conducting a medical camp, Dec. 11, 2025.
  • U.S. Army and Conco inaugurate new artillery container facility in Indiana Army News 06 Feb 2026 -- The U.S. Army and Conco, Inc. opened a new facility in Scottsburg, Indiana on Feb. 5, dedicated to producing metal packaging containers for the 155mm Modular Artillery Charge System (MACS). The ceremony, hosted by Maj. Gen. John T. Reim, Portfolio Acquisition Executive for Agile Sustainment and Ammunition, marks a critical step forward in expanding the nation's 155mm artillery ammunition production capacity and an enhancement of the nation's domestic industrial base. Scottsburg Mayor Terry Amick along with representatives from Senators Banks and Young and Congresswoman Houchin were also in attendance.
  • JIATF-401 Acquires Advanced Kinetic Counter-Drone System to Enhance Warfighter Lethality Army News 06 Feb 2026 -- Feb. 4, 2026 — The Joint Interagency Task Force 401 (JIATF-401) announced today it has awarded a $5.2 million agreement to Perennial Autonomy for the Bumblebee V2 counter-drone system to provide U.S. forces with a low-cost, low-collateral kinetic interceptor to defeat illicit drones at home and abroad.
  • Onboard USS Makin Island, 13th MEU and PHIBRON 7 Strengthen Navy-Marine Corps Integration During Amphibious Training USMC News 06 Feb 2026 -- The Navy-Marine Corps team has concluded Quarterly Underway Amphibious Readiness Training (QUART) 26.2, which took place from Jan. 20-30, 2026. The exercise, led by the 13th Marine Expeditionary Unit (MEU) and Amphibious Squadron (PHIBRON) 7, featured the Wasp-class amphibious assault ship USS Makin Island (LHD 8), which expanded the training's ability to prepare MEUs and Amphibious Ready Groups (ARGs) for future deployments and operations afloat. Further strengthening international partnerships, Peruvian naval officers were aboard to observe the exercise, fostering shared learning and military cooperation.
  • U.S. military, diplomatic leaders attend 2026 USEUCOM Chiefs of Mission Conference EUCOM 06 Feb 2026 -- U.S. European Command hosted its annual Chiefs of Mission conference at the command's headquarters Feb. 4-6 to discuss unified support to U.S. foreign policy and national security efforts in Europe. The conference gathers leaders from across Europe to discuss and synchronize diplomatic and military efforts across the European theater.

  • Swedish JAS 39 Gripen deployment to Iceland marks historic moment for NATO's newest Ally NATO Allied Air Command 06 Feb 2026 -- In a landmark deployment, six Swedish JAS 39 Gripen fighter aircraft and a contingent of more than 110 personnel from Skaraborg Air Wing F7 have arrived at Keflavík Air Base .

  • Ukraine - Class IX Spare Parts DSCA 06 Feb 2026 -- The State Department has made a determination approving a possible Foreign Military Sale to the Government of Ukraine of Class IX Spare Parts and related equipment for an estimated cost of $185 million. The Defense Security Cooperation Agency delivered the required certification notifying Congress.
  • The Short-Range Air Defense Component - Focused on Countering Attack Drones - Must Operate Far More Effectively and Prevent the Problems We Are Currently Facing - Address by the President President of Ukraine 06 Feb 2026
  • Canada to provide AIM missiles to Ukraine to counter cruise missiles Ukraine MOD 06 Feb 2026
  • F-16s, Patriot and joint manufacturing: Ukraine and Denmark strengthen defence cooperation Ukraine MOD 06 Feb 2026

  • Ministry of National Defense Issues Press Release Regarding "U.S. Government Formally Provides Draft Letters of Offer and Acceptance for Three Arms Sales Cases Including TOW Missile Follow-On Purchase" ROC MND 06 Feb 2026
  • President Lai Visits Xiaoxueshan Radar Station, Commends Armed Forces for Round-the-Clock Maritime Vigilance ROC MND 06 Feb 2026
  • ROC Armed Forces FY 2025 Accounting and Finance Review Conference; Vice Minister Hsu Commends Professional Excellence ROC MND 06 Feb 2026
  • CSBC aims to deliver first submarine prototype before end of June CNA 06 Feb 2026 -- Taiwanese shipbuilder CSBC Corp., Taiwan said Friday it aims to deliver the first domestically built submarine prototype, Hai Kun, before the end of June, after the vessel carried out its fourth submerged test earlier the same day.
  • Trump signs omnibus bill with over US$1.4 billion for Taiwan defense CNA 06 Feb 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump earlier this week signed a sweeping appropriations bill that includes more than US$1.4 billion to support security cooperation with Taiwan, among other measures.
  • Shaheen, Risch Statement on Taiwan's Special Defense Budget and Partnership with the United States US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 06 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senators Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH) and Jim Risch (R-ID), Ranking Member and Chairman of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the statement below regarding Taiwan's defense spending and partnership with the United States.
  • Taiwan plans 10-day Han Kuang drills, same as last year CNA 06 Feb 2026 -- This year's Han Kuang military exercises are to remain 10 days and nine nights, with a focus on decentralized command capabilities, operations in pre-designated kill zones and urban resilience, Defense Minister Wellington Koo (顧立雄) said.

  • Intermediate Range Ballistic Missile 'Agni-3' successfully test-fired India PIB 06 Feb 2026 -- Intermediate Range Ballistic Missile 'Agni-3' was successfully test-fired from the Integrated Test Range, Chandipur, Odisha on February 06, 2026
  • WESTERN AIR COMMAND HOSTS HIGH-LEVEL JOINT OPERATIONS CONCLAVE: ALL DOMAIN JOINT OPERATIONS (ADJO) EXERCISE 2026 FRAMEWORK India PIB 06 Feb 2026 -- Headquarters Western Air Command successfully conducted a high-level Joint Operations Conclave on 05 and 06 February 2026 under All Domain Joint Operations (ADJO) Exercise 2026 Framework

  • Nuclear disarmament at breaking point as mistrust grows - but hope remains UN News 06 Feb 2026 -- The global system governing nuclear disarmament is facing its most serious crisis in decades, driven by growing mistrust among major powers and the steady erosion of arms control agreements, a senior disarmament expert has warned.

  • National Armaments Director on business trip in Singapore and South Korea Federal Council - Swiss Government 06 Feb 2026 -- National Armaments Director Urs Loher was on a business trip in Singapore and South Korea from 2 to 6 February 2026. As part of this trip, meetings were held with various authorities, organisations and companies. In addition, the National Armaments Director visited the Singapore Airshow 2026.

  • Jordan: Jordan-EU Security and Defence Dialogue European External Action Service (EEAS) 06 Feb 2026

  • The Lithuanian Armed Forces restocks on artillery ammunition Lithuania MND 06 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of National Defence pursues a consistent plan of investment not just in modern military equipment but also ammunition supplies to keep strengthening national defence capabilities.

  • Tests of Unmanned Land Weapon Systems (UGVs) Poland - Ministry of National Defence 06 Feb 2026 -- The Unmanned Systems Forces Inspectorate announces the procedure for the acceptance of applications from manufacturers of unmanned solutions regarding the organization and testing modes in the Polish Armed Forces.

  • DND cites defense ties with Brazil, contribution to AFP modernization Philippine News Agency 06 Feb 2026 -- The Department of National Defense (DND) has expressed its appreciation to Brazil for being its only defense ally in Latin America and a major contributor to the ongoing Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) Modernization Program.

  • Deputy Secretary Landau's Meeting with Bosnia and Herzegovina Member of the Presidency Cvijanović US Dept. of State 06 Feb 2026
  • United States Participates in First APEC 2026 Senior Officials' Meeting in Guangzhou US Dept. of State 06 Feb 2026
  • Vance Heads to Armenia, Azerbaijan As US Pushes Peace, Trade, And Minerals Strategy RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- US Vice President JD Vance will visit Armenia and Azerbaijan to reinforce a US-brokered peace deal and advance a strategic transit corridor Washington sees as central to reshaping trade, energy, and influence across the South Caucasus.

Defense Industry

  • Contracts for Feb. 6, 2026

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Israeli keeps killing more Palestinian civilians in Gaza amid relentless ceasefire violations Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- At least two more Palestinians have been killed and another was wounded by Israeli fire across the Gaza Strip as the regime continues its near-daily violations of a ceasefire agreement with Palestinian resistance factions.

  • Concerns persist over herbicide spraying reports on Blue Line between Lebanon and Israel UN News 06 Feb 2026 -- The UN reiterated concerns on Friday over reports that Israeli forces sprayed a highly toxic herbicide over areas north of the Blue Line separating Lebanon from Israel on 1 February.
  • New Israeli operations in occupied West Bank raise serious concerns OHCHR 06 Feb 2026 -- A series of new Israeli operations and settlement plans in the occupied West Bank, including East Jerusalem, risk seriously undermining the viability of a Palestinian state and the realisation of the Palestinians' right to self-determination.

  • US imposes 'terrorist-grade sanctions' on UN expert, ICC judges amid Gaza accountability drive Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- The United States has imposed what critics describe as "terrorist-grade sanctions" on a United Nations human rights expert and multiple judges and prosecutors at the International Criminal Court (ICC), an investigative report has pointed out.

  • The 11th Japan-ROK Director-General Consultations on the Middle East Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 06 Feb 2026

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Sudan, Mali UNOCHA 06 Feb 2026

  • 2026 brings no respite to children living in violence and conflict in the Middle East and North Africa UNICEF 06 Feb 2026 -- Statement by UNICEF Regional Director for the Middle East and North Africa Edouard Beigbeder

  • On 4-5 February 2026, twenty four khwarij belonging to Indian Proxy Fitna Al Khwarij were killed in two separate engagements in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province. ISPR Pakistan 06 Feb 2026

  • France, UK involved in assassination of Muammar Gaddafi's son: Reports Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- France and the United Kingdom are reportedly involved in the assassination of Saif al-Islam Gaddafi, the prominent son of former Libyan President Muammar Gaddafi, as he was seen as the man who could reunite Libya after the NATO-backed uprising.
  • FBI Arrests Key Suspect in 2012 Benghazi Attack That Killed US Ambassador Sputnik 06 Feb 2026 -- The FBI has arrested a key suspect behind the September 11, 2012 attack on a US diplomatic mission and a nearby CIA annex in Libya's Benghazi that killed the US ambassador and three other Americans, Attorney General Pamela Bondi said on Friday.

  • Minister of Development condemns attack on hospital in South Sudan Government of Norway 06 Feb 2026 -- On 3 February, one of Doctors Without Borders/Médecins Sans Frontières' hospitals in Lankien, north-east South Sudan, was hit by an air strike carried out by government forces. The attack destroyed the hospital's main warehouse containing large quantities of vital medical supplies, and one employee was injured.

  • Ranking Member Shaheen Calls for Reimposition of Sanctions on Milorad Dodik US Senate Foreign Relations Committee 06 Feb 2026 -- Today, U.S. Senator Jeanne Shaheen (D-NH), Ranking Member of the Senate Foreign Relations Committee, released the below statement regarding the Trump Administration's decision to remove sanctions from Republika Srpska leader Milorad Dodik and calling for the reimposition of sanctions on Dodik.

  • Keeping hope 'alive for younger generations' in Haiti as funding falters UN News 06 Feb 2026 -- Haiti is facing one of the world's most acute humanitarian crises, driven by escalating gang violence, political paralysis, and deep economic distress.

News Reports

  • Presentation of this year's public threat and risk assessments Government of Norway 06 Feb 2026 -- The Norwegian Intelligence Service, the Police Security Service and the Norwegian National Security Authority today presented their public threat and risk assessments for 2026.

  • Senior Russian general shot and wounded in Moscow: Officials Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- A senior Russian military official who plays a major role in the country's intelligence services has been taken to hospital after being shot and wounded in Moscow.
  • Officials: Top Russian Intelligence General Shot, Wounded In Attempted Assassination RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- The No. 2 officer at the Russian military intelligence agency known as GRU was shot and wounded in Moscow, in what investigators called an assassination attempt.

  • Turkey arrests two on charges of spying for Israeli regime Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- Turkey has arrested two suspects on charges of spying for the Israeli regime.

  • The European Union (EU) and the United Nations (UN) convened their Seventh Dialogue on Counter-Terrorism in New York on 5 February 2026 European External Action Service (EEAS) 06 Feb 2026 -- Co-Chaired by the United Nations Office of Counter-Terrorism (UNOCT) and EEAS, in the framework of the EU-UN strategic partnership, the Dialogue offered the opportunity to exchange views on the global terrorist threat and to discuss strategic policy and coordination priorities related to their cooperation on counter terrorism and preventing and countering violent extremism, as and when, conducive to terrorism.

  • Commission responds to cyber-attack on its central mobile infrastructure European Commission 06 Feb 2026 -- On 30 January, the European Commission's central infrastructure managing mobile devices identified traces of a cyber-attack, which may have resulted in access to staff names and mobile numbers of some of its staff members.

  • 31 Killed In Suicide Attack On Shi'ite Mosque In Pakistani Capital RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- In one of the deadliest attacks in years, a suicide bomber killed at least 31 people during an attack on a Shi'ite Muslim mosque in Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, as it observed Friday Prayers.
  • Shia mosque explosion in Islamabad kills more than 30, injures over 160 Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- An explosion at a Shia mosque in Pakistan's capital Islamabad has killed more than 30 people and injured over 160 others, with officials warning the toll could rise.
  • Statement on the attack in Islamabad India MEA 06 Feb 2026
  • Iran condemns deadly terrorist attack in Islamabad IRNA 06 Feb 2026 -- Iran has condemned a terrorist attack against a mosque in the Pakistani capital city of Islamabad.
  • Iran strongly condemns 'terrorist' mosque blast in Islamabad Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- Iran has strongly condemned the deadly explosion at a Shia mosque in Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, which left more than 30 people dead and over 160 injured.
  • OIC General Secretariat Condemns the Terrorist Bombing that Targeted a Mosque in the Pakistani Capital Islamabad Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) 06 Feb 2026 -- The General Secretariat of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation (OIC) expresses its strong condemnation of the terrorist bombing that targeted a mosque in Islamabad, the capital of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, resulting in the loss of innocent lives
  • Saudi Arabia Condemns Terrorist Bombing of Mosque in Islamabad SPA 06 Feb 2026 -- The Ministry of Foreign Affairs expresses the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia's strong condemnation and denunciation of the terrorist bombing that targeted a mosque in the capital of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan, Islamabad, resulting in the death and injury of a number of people
  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Pakistan UN Secretary-General 06 Feb 2026
  • Pakistan: Guterres condemns deadly suicide bombing at Islamabad mosque UN News 06 Feb 2026 -- The UN Secretary-General on Friday strongly condemned an attack on worshippers at a mosque in Pakistan's capital, Islamabad, after an explosion during prayers reportedly killed dozens and injured many more.

  • Meeting with permanent members of the Security Council President of Russia 06 Feb 2026 -- Vladimir Putin chaired a briefing session with permanent members of the Security Council.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's opening remarks during talks with OSCE Chairperson-in-Office, Federal Councillor of the Swiss Confederation Ignazio Cassis and OSCE Secretary General Feridun Sinirlioglu, Moscow, February 6, 2026 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Feb 2026
  • MODIFYING DUTIES TO ADDRESS THREATS TO THE UNITED STATES BY THE GOVERNMENT OF THE RUSSIAN FEDERATION The White House 06 Feb 2026
  • OSCE Chairman-in-Office Cassis and Secretary General Sinirlioğlu visit Moscow, underscore need to end the war and OSCE's role as platform for dialogue OSCE 06 Feb 2026 -- The OSCE Chairman-in-Office, Switzerland's Federal Councillor and Head of the Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, Ignazio Cassis and OSCE Secretary General, Feridun H. Sinirlioğlu concluded a visit to Moscow today.
  • Statement by President von der Leyen on the 20th package of sanctions against Russia European Commission 06 Feb 2026 -- Russia's war of aggression against Ukraine will soon reach 1500 days. In the entire last year, Russian forces advanced on average between 15 and 70 metres per day.
  • Foreign Ministry delivers a note to the chargé d'affaires ad interim of the Russian Embassy on the night-time closure of border crossing points Estonia MFA 06 Feb 2026 -- Today, 6 February, the Estonian Ministry of Foreign Affairs summoned the chargé d'affaires ad interim of the Russian Embassy and delivered a diplomatic note informing them that Estonia will close two border crossing points to night-time traffic.

  • Chinese embassy in France says it is yet to receive notification from France on probe into alleged spying involving Chinese nationals Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- In response to some media reports claiming that French authorities on Thursday charged four people, including two Chinese nationals, on suspicion of having intercepted sensitive military data for Beijing, the Chinese Embassy in France told the Global Times on Friday that it has taken note of relevant reports by French media. It has not yet received any notification from the French side.
  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Lin Jian's Regular Press Conference on February 6, 2026 PRC MofA 06 Feb 2026
  • China's position on Panama port issue is clear: FM on reported move to request Chinese SOEs to suspend new project talks with Panama Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- Asked to comment on reports that China has requested state-owned enterprises to suspend talks on new cooperation projects with Panama, Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Friday at the regular press conference that China has previously stated its position on the relevant port issue in Panama on multiple occasions. "Our position is clear," Lin said, adding that for specific details, the inquirer should consult the relevant Chinese authorities.
  • China launches BeiDou-based satellite short messaging service for emergency needs People's Daily 06 Feb 2026 -- China has launched a new satellite short messaging service leveraging the BeiDou Navigation Satellite System (BDS), designed to provide reliable communication during emergencies when ground-based mobile networks are unavailable.

  • Iran Calls Diplomatic Talks With US 'A Good Start' RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- Iran says a round of talks in Oman with the United States was a good start as the two sides look to overcome a "wall of mistrust" to bring down rising tensions that have sparked fears of a major military escalation in the Persian Gulf.
  • Indirect Iran-US talks end 'for now': media; FM spokesperson says China maintains communication with parties on relevant issues Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- Indirect talks between Iran and the US in Oman's Muscat ended "for now," and the two delegations may return home, Iran's state-run IRIB TV reported Friday, according to the Xinhua News Agency.
  • Iran FM declares 'good start' as US-Iran talks conclude in Muscat Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says the new round of indirect nuclear talks with the United States in the Omani capital Muscat was a "good start" and can be continued.
  • Iran-US talks in Oman focus solely on nuclear issue: Envoy Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- The head of Iran's Interests Section in Egypt has emphasized that the negotiations between Tehran and Washington in Oman will focus solely on the nuclear issue.
  • Note to Correspondents: Note from the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on the US/Iran talks UN Secretary-General 06 Feb 2026
  • Guterres welcomes resumption of Iran-US talks UN News 06 Feb 2026 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Friday welcomed the resumption of talks between Iran and the United States.
  • Araghchi: Iran ready to defend sovereignty and national security against adventurism IRNA 06 Feb 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and his Omani counterpart Sayyid Badr bin Hamad al-Busaidi have discussed key bilateral, regional, and international issues.
  • ADDRESSING THREATS TO THE UNITED STATES BY THE GOVERNMENT OF IRAN The White House 06 Feb 2026
  • Fact Sheet: President Donald J. Trump Addresses Threats to the United States by the Government of Iran The White House 06 Feb 2026
  • Sanctions to Combat Illicit Traders of Iranian Oil and the Shadow Fleet US Dept. of State 06 Feb 2026 -- The Department of State is sanctioning 15 entities, two individuals, and 14 shadow fleet vessels connected to the illicit trade in Iranian petroleum, petroleum products, and petrochemical products. These targets have generated revenue that the regime uses to conduct its malign activities.
  • Sanctions to Combat Illicit Traders of Iranian Oil and the Shadow Fleet US Dept. of State 06 Feb 2026 -- Fact Sheet
  • Egyptian FM, IAEA's Grossi discuss Iran's nuclear file IRNA 06 Feb 2026 -- Egypt's Foreign Minister Badr Abdelatty and IAEA Director General Rafael Grossi have discussed the latest developments in Iran's nuclear program and potential pathways to resume negotiations.
  • China reaffirms support for Iran's nuclear rights amid Iran-US talks Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- China has once again voiced support for Iran's right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy and stressed that Tehran's nuclear issue must be resolved through political and diplomatic means.
  • Aliyev: Azerbaijani territory will not be used for threats against Iran Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- Azerbaijan's President Ilham Aliyev has said his country will not allow its territory to be used for any threat against Iran, amid US threats of military aggression against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iran defense minister visits Azerbaijan to boost security, defense ties Press TV 06 Feb 2026 -- Iran's Defense Minister Brigadier General Aziz Nasirzadeh has traveled to Baku for talks with Azerbaijani officials.
  • Diplomatic Showdown Between US And Iran Set For Oman RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- Iranian and US officials are set to meet in Oman on February 6 for a round of high-stakes diplomacy seen as a last-chance effort to avert a major military escalation in the Persian Gulf.
  • Upcoming Iran-US negotiations in Oman solely on nuclear issue: Iran's envoy IRNA 06 Feb 2026 -- The head of interests section of Iran in Egypt, Mojtaba Ferdowsipour, has emphasized that the negotiations between Iran and the United States in Oman on February 6 will focus solely on the nuclear issue.
  • Chinese Vice FM Miao Deyu meets with Iranian counterpart, says China supports Iran in safeguarding sovereignty, opposes unilateral bullying Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- Chinese Vice Foreign Minister Miao Deyu met with Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs Kazem Gharibabadi in Beijing on Thursday, according to the Chinese Foreign Ministry.
  • Chinese, Iranian diplomats meet before US-Iran nuclear talk; move reflects Teheran's willingness to seek intl support: Chinese expert Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- Just a day before nuclear talks between Iran and the US is set to take place in Oman, Chinese Assistant Foreign Minister Liu Bin met with visiting Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs Kazem Gharibabadi on Thursday, as the two sides focused their discussions on exchanging views regarding the current situation of the Iran nuclear issue. The Chinese side reiterated its advocacy for the peaceful resolution of the Iran nuclear issue, while stressing that China supports Iran's legitimate right to the peaceful use of nuclear energy.

  • China urges Lithuania to translate wish to improve ties into action, correct mistake on Taiwan question: FM Global Times 06 Feb 2026 -- China hopes Lithuania will translate its wish to improve bilateral relations into concrete actions, correct its mistakes as soon as possible, return to the right track of earnestly adhering to the one-China principle, in order to create necessary conditions for the normalization of China-Lithuania relations, Chinese Foreign Ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said on Friday, in response to a question whether China considers fully restoring diplomatic ties with Lithuania and what conditions need to be met for the restoration of relations after Lithuanian Prime Minister Inga Ruginienė said on Tuesday that allowing Taiwan regional authorities to establish a so-called "Taiwan representative office" in its capital Vilnius was a strategic mistake.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 06 Feb 2026

  • JOINT DECLARATION ON THE OUTCOMES OF THE STATE VISIT OF THE PRESIDENT OF THE REPUBLIC OF UZBEKISTAN TO THE ISLAMIC REPUBLIC OF PAKISTAN Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Feb 2026 -- The President of the Republic of Uzbekistan His Excellency Shavkat Mirziyoyev paid a State visit to the Islamic Republic of Pakistan on 5-6 February 2026, at the invitation of the Prime Minister of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan His Excellency Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif.

  • Joint media statement - Jakarta, Indonesia Prime Minister of Australia 06 Feb 2026

  • Minister Anand meets with Greenland's Minister of Foreign Affairs and Research Canada 06 Feb 2026
  • Minister Anand meets with Denmark's Minister for Foreign Affairs Canada 06 Feb 2026

  • Expiry of the New START Treaty (6 February 2026) France Ministry for Europe and Foreign Affairs 06 Feb 2026 -- The New START Treaty, the latest strategic arms control treaty between the United States and Russia, expired on 5 February 2026.

  • Chancellor's trip to Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates Strengthening strategic partnerships Germany Federal Foreign Office 06 Feb 2026 -- Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the United Arab Emirates are key players in the Near and Middle East. Federal Chancellor Merz has travelled to the Gulf region to strengthen strategic partnerships. Developments in Iran were also a topic of discussion.

  • Joint Statement by Turkish Foreign Minister H.E. Hakan Fidan and EU Commissioner for Enlargement H.E. Marta Kos following their Meeting in Ankara, 6 February 2026 Republic of Turkey Ministry of Foreign Affairs 06 Feb 2026

  • Budrys meets Sandu in Chișinău: We cannot allow the Kremlin's narratives to win Lithuania MFA 06 Feb 2026 -- On 6 February in Chișinău, the Minister of Foreign Affairs of Lithuania, Kęstutis Budrys, met with the President of Moldova, Maia Sandu. The meeting focused on bilateral relations, Moldova's EU integration process, and strengthening resilience to Russian hybrid attacks.

  • Kyrgyzstan May Be First State Targeted Under EU Anti-Circumvention Rules RFE/RL 06 Feb 2026 -- Under a proposed 20th package of sanctions against Russia, which are expected to be introduced by the end of February, Kyrgyzstan could starting facing certain export restrictions.
